DISCOVERY, 302, MOTION FOR ORDER COMPELLING FURTHER RESPONSE TO INTERROGATORIES IS DENIED AS TO FORM INTERROGATORY 9.1, AND SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES 43 AND 46. AS TO SPECIAL INTERROGATORY 74, PLAINTIFF WILL SERVE A FURTHER RESPONSE BY SEPTEMBER 25, 2014. AS TO SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES 105 AND 106, FURTHER RESPONSES WILL BE SERVED TWO WEEKS AFTER A STIPULATED PROTECTIVE ORDER IS SIGNED BY JUDGE HYDE. COUNSEL TO PREPARE A STIPULATED PROTECTIVE ORDER. THE MATTER IS CONTINUED TO OCTOBER 9, 2014 IN CASE THE PARTIES ARE UNABLE TO REACH AGREEMENT ON THE TERMS OF THE PROTECTIVE ORDER OR OTHERWISE. ORDER SIGNED. MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Jun-29-2015 Vacated

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Notice Of Defendants' Motion For Summary Judgment Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication
Off Calendar Jun-18-2015 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances in the matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANTS PATRICK RILEY, AND ARK.COM, INC.'S Motion For Summary Judgment Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication is taken off calendar. A dismissal of the entire case was filed on May 4, 2015. Judge Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk Felicia Green; Dept. 302. (302/EHG)

Notice Of Motion For Leave To A File Cross-Complaint
Off Calendar Mar-12-2015 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Stuart G. Gross, Esq. of Gross Law PC, Tel. 415.671.4628, for defendant and moving party;
Parveen K. Tumber, Esq. of Tumber Law Firm, Tel. 415.874.3330, for plaintiff and opposing party.

RULING: The court required a hearing on DEFENDANT ARK.COM, INC.'s Motion For Leave To A File Cross-Complaint. The matter was argued. At the conclusion of argument, the motion is denied. The Court finds that the motion is dilatory, highly prejudicial, not in good faith and not timely, as the case has been going on for 2 years. (Prevailing party to prepare a proposed order.) Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Reporter: Melanie Gheno, CSR# 7489.

Notice Of Motion For Leave To A File Cross-Complaint
Off Calendar Feb-13-2015 Continued To Mar-12-15 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT ARK.COM, INC. Motion For Leave To A File Cross-Complaint is continued to March 12, 2015 to give opposing party the opportunity to comply with Local Rule 2.6B. Opposing party must deliver courtesy copies of opposition papers to Department 302, with a cover letter stating the new hearing date, no later than February 13, 2015 at 4:00 p.m. or the motion will be granted. Any exhibit must be separated by an exhibit tab per CRC 3.1110(f). The Court has courtesy copies of the moving and reply papers. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Clerk: Sean Kane, Not Reported. (302/EHG)

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Compelling Further Response To Interrogatories And Imposing Monetary Sanction
Off Calendar Sep-11-2014 Continued To Oct-09-14 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Parveen K. Tumber, of Tumber Law Firm, Tel. 916.572.0414, for plaintiffs and opposing party;
Stuart G. Gross, of Gross Law, Tel. 415.671.4628, for defendant and moving party;
No other appearances.

DEFENDANT ARK.COM, INC. Motion For Order Compelling Further Response To Interrogatories And Imposing Monetary Sanction is denied as to Form Interrogatory 9.1, and Special Interrogatories 43 and 46. As to Special Interrogatory 74, plaintiff will serve a further response by September 25, 2014. As to Special Interrogatories 105 and 106, further responses will be served two weeks after a stipulated protective order is signed by Judge Hyde. Counsel to prepare a stipulated protective order. The matter is continued to October 9, 2014 in case the parties are unable to reach agreement on the terms of the protective order or otherwise. ORDER SIGNED. Judge Pro Tem: Dennis Hyde; Clerk: Felicia Green; Not reported; Dept. 302.

Notice Of Motion For Award Of Sanctions For Actions Under California Code Of Civil Procedure Section 128.7
Off Calendar Sep-09-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
DEFENDANTS ARK.COM, INC.'S Motion For Award Of Sanctions For Actions Under California Code Of Civil Procedure Section 128.7 is Denied. Defendant has not established that Plaintiff's counsel filed a document with an improper purpose. Defendant provides no evidence that shows that Plaintiff's counsel knew that personal service was not properly effectuated. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Sheryl L. Sawyer, CSR # 5976 =302/eg
